steps.title: Locate the finding that creates the puzzle
steps.content: Desai et al. found that “democratic strength, if associated with low inequality, restrains inflationary pressures.” This creates a puzzle: Canada has both traits, so the finding predicts lower inflation, yet Canada's 2.26% inflation exceeds Oman's 0.88%.

steps.title: Apply the finding to the choices
steps.content:
If Desai et al.'s finding is correct but Canada's data contradicts its prediction, then factors beyond political structure and inequality must explain the difference. Choice C concludes that other factors contributed to Canada's higher inflation—directly resolving the contradiction.

The other choices either challenge the foundational data, resurrect a contradicted theory, or oversimplify the finding's scope.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
